text:
Layer 1: Core Premise
●​ Summer vacation adventures of Phineas and Ferb​ Layer 2: Main Characters
●​ Phineas Flynn​ ○​ Creative, optimistic, idea generator​ ●​ Ferb Fletcher​ ○​ Quiet, resourceful, skilled builder​ ●​ Candace Flynn​ ○​ Older sister, obsessed with "busting" brothers​ ●​ Linda Flynn-Fletcher (Mom)​ ○​ Never sees the inventions in time​ ●​ Perry the Platypus / Agent P​ ○​ Secret agent double life​ ●​ Dr. Heinz Doofenshmirtz​ ○​ Villain, creator of absurd "-inators"​ Layer 3: Story Structure & Themes
●​ Phineas and Ferb's Projects​ ○​ Huge inventions (rollercoasters, time machines, spaceships)​ ○​ Embody creativity and imagination​ ●​ Candace's Subplot​ ○​ Constant attempts to tattle to Mom​ ○​ Always thwarted by timing or coincidence​ ●​ Perry vs. Doofenshmirtz​ ○​ Spy battles run parallel to boys' adventures​ ○​ Doofenshmirtz's failures usually erase evidence of the inventions​ ●​ Recurring Humor & Gags​ ○​ "Ferb, I know what we're gonna do today!"​ ○​ Doofenshmirtz's tragic backstories​ ○​ Candace yelling "Mooooom!"​ Layer 4: Underlying Themes
●​ Celebration of creativity and imagination​ ●​ Sibling dynamics (supportive brothers vs. frustrated sister)​ ●​ Double lives and hidden worlds (Perry as Agent P)​ ●​ Absurd humor with heart (villain's failures, family comedy)

○​ "Ferb, I know what we're gonna do today!"​ ○​ Doofenshmirtz's tragic backstories​ ○​ Candace yelling "Mooooom!"​ Layer 4: Underlying Themes
●​ Celebration of creativity and imagination​ ●​ Sibling dynamics (supportive brothers vs. frustrated sister)​ ●​ Double lives and hidden worlds (Perry as Agent P)​ ●​ Absurd humor with heart (villain's failures, family comedy)

○​ Candace yelling "Mooooom!"​ Layer 4: Underlying Themes
●​ Celebration of creativity and imagination​ ●​ Sibling dynamics (supportive brothers vs. frustrated sister)​ ●​ Double lives and hidden worlds (Perry as Agent P)​ ●​ Absurd humor with heart (villain's failures, family comedy)

K-Pop: A Global Phenomenon
K-pop, or Korean popular music, is a music genre that originated in South Korea in the early
1990s. It is characterized by a wide variety of audiovisual elements, including synchronized
choreography, elaborate music videos, and distinctive fashion. The Big Four Entertainment Companies
The K-pop industry is largely controlled by four major entertainment companies: SM
Entertainment, JYP Entertainment, YG Entertainment, and HYBE. These companies are
responsible for training, producing, and managing the careers of idol groups. SM Entertainment was founded by Lee Soo-man in 1989 and is home to groups like EXO, NCT,
aespa, and Girls' Generation. JYP Entertainment, founded by Park Jin-young, manages TWICE,
Stray Kids, ITZY, and GOT7. YG Entertainment is known for BLACKPINK and BIGBANG. HYBE, formerly Big Hit Entertainment, is the agency behind BTS and NewJeans. The Idol Training System
Aspiring K-pop idols undergo years of rigorous training in singing, dancing, languages, and
performance before debuting. Training periods can last anywhere from one to ten years. Trainees are recruited through auditions held globally, reflecting the international ambitions of
the industry. BTS and Global Expansion
BTS, a seven-member boy group formed by HYBE in 2013, became the first K-pop act to top
the Billboard Hot 100. The group's members -- RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V, and Jungkook
-- each pursued solo careers beginning in 2022 while members fulfilled mandatory South
Korean military service. BLACKPINK
BLACKPINK, consisting of members Jisoo, Jennie, Rosé, and Lisa, debuted under YG
Entertainment in 2016. They became the first K-pop girl group to perform at Coachella in 2019. Each member has since launched successful solo careers internationally. The Hallyu Wave
The global spread of Korean culture, known as the Hallyu or Korean Wave, encompasses
K-pop, K-dramas, Korean cinema, and Korean food. K-pop has been a major driver of this
wave, leading to increased interest in the Korean language and culture worldwide. Fandoms K-pop fandoms are highly organized and passionate communities. BTS fans are called ARMY,
BLACKPINK fans are called Blinks, and TWICE fans are called Once. Fans play an active role
in charting songs by streaming music, buying albums, and voting in music shows.

Taylor Swift is a pop artist. She released Folklore in 2020 and Midnights in 2022. She is
managed by Tree Paine. She dated Jake Gyllenhaal and Travis Kelce. Travis Kelce plays for
the Kansas City Chiefs in the NFL. Folklore was produced by Aaron Dessner and Jack
Antonoff. Jack Antonoff also worked with Lana Del Rey and Lorde.
